title: docs_review_verdict
description: Verdict produced by the docs-review story's agent.decide call. Pinned into world.verdict and written to .artifacts/docs-review-<sha>.md. The shape is designed to render as a listing: one row per (doc, line-range), with 1+ invalidation records explaining the code/doc change that broke that section.

decision.description: Binary verdict — needs_update means at least one doc section is stale.

summary.description: One-paragraph rationale; cites file paths, line ranges, and commit short-shas.

commits.description: Every commit considered in this audit. Used as the lookup table referenced by stale_docs[].invalidations[].commit. May be empty only when the verdict fell back to a structural audit.

change.description: One sentence on what observable behavior or surface this commit changed. Not a restatement of the subject.

stale_docs.description: One record per (doc, line-range) that is now stale. Empty array when decision == up_to_date.

lines.description: Line range inside the doc, e.g. '110-117' or '650' for a single line. Required — the listing is keyed by (path, lines).

anchor.description: Optional human-readable anchor, e.g. '§4.3 Iterate on an app with full tracing'.

invalidations.description: 1+ records of what changed (in code, or another doc) such that this doc section is now wrong or incomplete. One change per record.

commit.description: SHA from commits[], or the literal 'working-tree' for uncommitted changes, or 'structural' when no specific commit applies.

source.description: Optional file path that drives the invalidation (e.g. 'cmd/kitsoki/trace.go' or 'docs/architecture.md'). Cite when it sharpens the link.

change.description: What the commit / source changed at the behavior or contract level.

reason.description: Why this change makes the cited doc section stale. Quote or paraphrase the doc's current claim.

recommended_actions.description: Short directive strings, ideally one per stale_docs[] entry in the same order.
